objects.U_Q: set of residues r (mod Q) such that both r and r+2 are coprime to Q, for squarefree Q with 6|Q
objects.B_Q(x): sum_{r in U_Q} min(pi(x;Q,r), pi(x;Q,r+2)), residue-pairing bound
objects.pi(x;Q,a): count of primes p <= x with p equiv a (mod Q)

objects.B_Q(x)_identity: B_Q(x) = (1/2) sum_{r in U_Q} (a_Q(r;x) + a_Q(r+2;x) - |a_Q(r;x) - a_Q(r+2;x)|)

objects.F_{Q_2 -> Q_1}(r_1): {r_2 in U_{Q_2} : r_2 equiv r_1 (mod Q_1)}, fiber for Q_1 | Q_2

objects.h-step_(m+1)-tuple: a set of primes {p, p+h, ..., p+mh} for h in 2N and m >= 1
objects.U_Q^{(h,m)}: {r (mod Q) : gcd(r+jh,Q)=1 for all j=0,...,m}, admissible starts for modulus Q >= 2
objects.M_Q^{(h,m)}(r;x): min_{0<=j<=m} a_Q(r+jh;x), tuple-minimum over fixed start r
objects.B_Q^{(h,m)}(x): sum_{r in U_Q^{(h,m)}} M_Q^{(h,m)}(r;x), modular minima bound

conjectures.statement: For all x >= 7 and any squarefree Q with 6|Q: pi_2(x) << B_Q(x).

conjectures.name: Monotone refinement
conjectures.statement: If Q_1 | Q_2 are both squarefree and divisible by 6, then for every x >= 7: B_{Q_2}(x) <= B_{Q_1}(x). This implies that as we refine the modular partition, the sum of modular minima is non-increasing.

Another computationally sharp inequality is:
pi_2(x) << (1/2) S_Q(x) - (1/2) V_Q(x),
where S_Q(x) := sum_{r in U_Q} (a_Q(r;x) + a_Q(r+2;x)) is the total mass in admissible classes and V_Q(x) := sum_{r in U_Q} |a_Q(r;x) - a_Q(r+2;x)| is the summed class-discrepancy.

A coarser pi-only corollary replaces (1/2)(a_Q(r;x)+a_Q(r+2;x)) by min(a_Q(r;x),a_Q(r+2;x)) <= (1/2)(a_Q(r;x)+a_Q(r+2;x)), eliminating explicit absolute values:
pi_2(x) <= 2 + (1/2) (pi(x) - sum_{a in F_Q} pi(x;Q,a) - c_Q),
where F_Q := {a (mod Q) : gcd(a,Q)=1 but gcd(a+2,Q) != 1} are forbidden start-classes and c_Q removes the small primes dividing Q.

If Q_1 | Q_2 are squarefree with 6|Q_1, then for all x >= 7:
B_{Q_2}(x) <= sum_{r_1 in U_{Q_1}} min(sum_{r_2 in F} a_{Q_2}(r_2;x), sum_{r_2 in F} a_{Q_2}(r_2+2;x)) <= B_{Q_1}(x),
where F = F_{Q_2->Q_1}(r_1).

conjectures.name: Exact fiber cancellation identity (Gap decomposition)
conjectures.statement:
For each r_1 in U_{Q_1}, let L(r_1;x) and R(r_1;x) be the sums of the left and right counts in the fiber. The fiber's cancellation deficit is:
delta_{r_1}(x) := (1/2) [sum_{r_2 in F} |d_{r_2}(x)| - |sum_{r_2 in F} d_{r_2}(x)|] >= 0,
where d_{r_2}(x) = a_{Q_2}(r_2;x) - a_{Q_2}(r_2+2;x). The total gap splits as:
B_{Q_1}(x) - B_{Q_2}(x) = Phi_{Q_1 <- Q_2}(x) + sum_{r_1 in U_{Q_1}} delta_{r_1}(x),
where Phi is the loss from residue classes that become forbidden modulo Q_2.

conjectures.name: Strict Drop
conjectures.statement:
Fix squarefree Q and prime p >= 5 coprime to Q. There exists X_0 such that for all x >= X_0:
B_{pQ}(x) <= B_Q(x) - 1 (SD).
This predicts a persistent strict drop along primorial refinement due to the emergence of forbidden residue families and fiber imbalances.

For all x >= 7:
N_{h,m}(x) <= C_{h,m}(Q) + B_Q^{(h,m)}(x) (*),
where N_{h,m}(x) counts h-step (m+1)-tuples with p+mh <= x, and C_{h,m}(Q) is a small explicit constant accounting for tuples involving small primes dividing Q.

conjectures.name: Example: Cousin primes (h=4), m=1
conjectures.statement:
Q=6: admissible start r equiv 1 (mod 6); end r+4 equiv 5 (mod 6). For x >= 7:
pi_4(x) <= 1 + min{pi(x;6,1), pi(x;6,5)}.
The constant 1 accounts for the exceptional pair (3,7).

objects.pi_2(x): #{p <= x-2 : p and p+2 are prime}
objects.pi_4(x): #{p <= x-4 : p and p+4 are prime}
objects.pi(x;q,a): count of primes <= x that are equiv a (mod q)

objects.pi_h(x): #{p <= x-h : p and p+h are prime} for even h >= 2
objects.pi(x;q,a): #{p <= x : p prime, p equiv a (mod q)}
objects.B_{q,h}(x): (1/log x) sum_{a (mod q)} K_{q,h}(a) * Delta_q(a;x), mod-q pair-bias transform for gap h
objects.R_{q;h_1,h_2}(x): B_{q,h_1}(x) - B_{q,h_2}(x) = (1/log x) sum_{a (mod q)} (K_{q,h_1}(a) - K_{q,h_2}(a)) * Delta_q(a;x)

objects.pi_h(x): #{p <= x-h : p and p+h are prime} for even h >= 0, pi_0(x) = pi(x)
objects.B_{6,h}(x): (1/log x) * sum_{a (mod 6)} K_{6,h}(a) * Delta_6(a;x), bias transform

objects.pi_h(x): #{p <= x-h : p and p+h are prime} for even h >= 0, pi_0(x) = pi(x)
objects.pi(x;q,a): #{p <= x : p prime, p equiv a (mod q)}
objects.B_{q,h}(a;x): (1/log x) * K_{q,h}(a) * Delta_q(a;x), residuewise bias transform
objects.F_{q;2,4}(x): sum_{a (mod q)} min(B_{q,2}(a;x), B_{q,4}(a;x)), min-bias aggregator

definitions.description: For q squarefree, q equiv 0 (mod 6), x >= 7:

objects.K_{q,2}: 1 on reduced residues a equiv 5 (mod 6), else 0
objects.K_{q,4}: 1 on reduced residues a equiv 1 (mod 6), else 0
objects.omega_q^{(1)}(x): count of prime divisors of q <= x that are equiv 1 (mod 6)
objects.omega_q^{(5)}(x): count of prime divisors of q <= x that are equiv 5 (mod 6)

basic_properties.P1_Linearity: B_{q,h}(x) is linear in the vector (Delta_q(a;x))_a, and R_{q;h_1,h_2} is linear in K-differences.
basic_properties.P2_Locality_CRT: If q = prod p_i is squarefree, then K_{q,h}(a) = prod_i 1_{a not equiv 0,-h (mod p_i)} and B_{q,h} factors across primes dividing q.
basic_properties.P3_Stability_under_refinement: If q|Q (both squarefree), then the pushforward of weights from modulo Q to modulo q preserves R in the sense that sum_{b equiv a (mod q)} (K_{Q,h_1}-K_{Q,h_2})(b) = (K_{q,h_1}-K_{q,h_2})(a).
basic_properties.P4_Twin_cousin_universality: For any squarefree q equiv 0 (mod 6), identity (3) holds; thus the twin-cousin discrepancy depends only on the prime-race pi(x;6,5) vs. pi(x;6,1) at main bias scale.
basic_properties.P5_Scale: Under standard distribution in AP on average, the error x/(log x)^3 is natural for sieve-based pair counts.

conjectures.name: Structural inequality
conjectures.statement: For all x >= 7: pi_2(x) + pi_4(x) <= pi(x-4) + 2. This follows from the impossibility of prime triplets {p,p+2,p+4} beyond {3,5,7}.

conjectures.name: Twin-cousin bias inequality
conjectures.statement:
There exist absolute constants c_1, c_2 > 0 and x_0 such that for all x >= x_0:
c_1 * (pi(x;3,2) - pi(x;3,1)) / log x - x/(log x)^3 <= pi_2(x) - pi_4(x) <= c_2 * (pi(x;3,2) - pi(x;3,1)) / log x + x/(log x)^3.
In particular, the set of x for which pi_2(x) > pi_4(x) has logarithmic density strictly larger than 1/2.

conjectures.name: Structural obstruction inequality
conjectures.statement:
Let r >= 3 be an odd prime. Then for all x >= 2r:
sum_{j=1}^{r-1} pi_{2j}(x) <= (r-2) * pi(x-2(r-1)) + O_r(1).
In particular, for r=3 this yields pi_2(x) + pi_4(x) <= pi(x-4) + O(1), recovering and sharpening the classical mod-3 obstruction.

conjectures.name: Universal mod-q pair-bias inequality
conjectures.statement:
Let q >= 3 be squarefree and h_1, h_2 >= 2 be even. Then there exists C(q,h_1,h_2) > 0 and x_0 such that for all x >= x_0:
|pi_{h_1}(x) - pi_{h_2}(x) - R_{q;h_1,h_2}(x)| <= C(q,h_1,h_2) * x/(log x)^3.
Equivalently, the sign and size of pi_{h_1}(x) - pi_{h_2}(x) at scale << x/(log x)^2 are governed by the weighted prime-race imbalance R_{q;h_1,h_2}(x).

conjectures.name: Concrete Specialization
conjectures.statement:
For any q multiple of 6, K_{q,2}-K_{q,4} is supported on classes a with a equiv 1 or 5 (mod 6) and equals -1 on a equiv 1 (mod 6), +1 on a equiv 5 (mod 6). Hence:
R_{q;2,4}(x) = (1/log x) (pi(x;6,5) - pi(x;6,1)).
Thus (the Universal mod-q pair-bias inequality) predicts that pi_2(x)-pi_4(x) tracks the classical Chebyshev bias between 5 and 1 modulo 6, uniformly across all squarefree moduli q divisible by 6.

conjectures.name: Exact Triad Identities
conjectures.statement:
(I1) B_{6,2}(x) = Delta_6(5;x)/log x, B_{6,4}(x) = Delta_6(1;x)/log x, B_{6,0}(x) = (Delta_6(1;x)+Delta_6(5;x))/log x.
(I2) Mass-conservation and race-duality:
B_{6,2}(x) + B_{6,4}(x) = B_{6,0}(x) = -omega_6(x)/log x.
B_{6,2}(x) - B_{6,4}(x) = (pi(x;6,5)-pi(x;6,1))/log x.

conjectures.name: Elementary Start/End Class Caps
conjectures.statement:
For all x >= 7:
(C1) Start-side caps: pi_2(x) <= pi(x-2;6,5)+1, pi_4(x) <= pi(x-4;6,1)+1.
(C2) End-side caps: pi_2(x) <= pi(x;6,1)+1, pi_4(x) <= pi(x;6,5)+1.
(O) Aggregate Obstruction: pi_2(x) + pi_4(x) <= pi(x-4) + 2.

conjectures.name: Conjectural Bias-Inequality
conjectures.statement:
There exists an absolute C > 0 and x_0 such that for all x >= x_0:
|pi_2(x) - pi_4(x) - (pi(x;6,5) - pi(x;6,1))/log x| <= C * x/(log x)^3.
Equivalently, the twin-cousin discrepancy is governed by the explicit prime race between 5 and 1 modulo 6 at amplitude 1/log x, up to a sieve-scale error.

For any modulus q divisible by 6 and all x >= 7:
F_{q;2,4}(x) = -(omega_q(x) + T_q(x)) / (2 * log x),
where T_q(x) := sum_{a (mod q), gcd(a,q)=1} |Delta_q(a;x)| is the total variation of the centered prime counts across all reduced residue classes modulo q. In particular, F_{q;2,4}(x) <= 0 and becomes more negative as either omega_q(x) or the dispersion T_q(x) increases.

conjectures.name: Simple Universal Bounds and Equality Cases
conjectures.statement:
(B1) Two-sided bounds:
-(|d(x)| + 2 omega_q(x)) / (2*log x) <= F_{q;2,4}(x) <= -omega_q(x)/log x <= 0,
where d(x) := pi(x;6,5) - pi(x;6,1) (q-independent for q multiple of 6).
(B2) Sharper sandwich (q=6): With B_{6,0}(x) := -omega_6(x)/log x and R(x) := (pi(x;6,5)-pi(x;6,1))/log x:
B_{6,0}(x) - |R(x)|/2 <= F_{6;2,4}(x) <= B_{6,0}(x) <= 0.

Define S_q(x) := B_{q,2}(x) + B_{q,4}(x) = -omega_q(x)/log x, and
D_q(x) := B_{q,2}(x) - B_{q,4}(x) = (pi(x;6,5) - pi(x;6,1) - (omega_q^{(5)}(x) - omega_q^{(1)}(x))) / log x.
Then B_{q,2}(x) = (S_q(x)+D_q(x))/2 and B_{q,4}(x) = (S_q(x)-D_q(x))/2.

conjectures.name: Ellipse law
conjectures.statement:
(B_{q,2}(x) - S_q(x)/2)^2 + (B_{q,4}(x) - S_q(x)/2)^2 = D_q(x)^2/2.
In particular, the pair (B_{q,2}, B_{q,4}) lies on a circle of radius |D_q|/sqrt(2) centered at (S_q/2, S_q/2), so |B_{q,2} - B_{q,4}| = |D_q|.

conjectures.name: Product identity and bounds
conjectures.statement:
4 B_{q,2}(x) B_{q,4}(x) = S_q(x)^2 - D_q(x)^2.
Hence:
-D_q(x)^2/4 <= B_{q,2}(x) B_{q,4}(x) <= S_q(x)^2/4 = omega_q(x)^2 / (4 log^2 x).
Writing the total variation T_q(x) := sum_{(a,q)=1} |Delta_q(a;x)|, we have |D_q(x)| <= T_q(x)/log x, so:
(S_q(x)^2 - (T_q(x)/log x)^2) / 4 <= B_{q,2}(x) B_{q,4}(x) <= S_q(x)^2/4.

conjectures.name: L^1/L^2 control of the race
conjectures.statement:
Let chi_6(a) = +1 on a equiv 5 (mod 6), -1 on a equiv 1 (mod 6), lifted to modulo q.
|D_q(x)| <= T_q(x)/log x.
|D_q(x)| <= (1/log x) sqrt(phi(q) * V_q(x)), where V_q(x) := sum_{(a,q)=1} Delta_q(a;x)^2.

conjectures.name: Sum and max of absolute values
One-sided sign constraints and bracketing
conjectures.statement:
Since S_q(x) <= 0, at least one of B_{q,2}(x), B_{q,4}(x) is <= 0. Furthermore:
min(B_{q,2}(x), B_{q,4}(x)) <= S_q(x)/2 <= max(B_{q,2}(x), B_{q,4}(x)).

objects.pi_h(x): #{p <= x-h : p and p+h are prime} for even h >= 2 with 3 not | h
objects.r(h): unique residue class mod 6 such that p equiv r(h) (mod 6) forces p and p+h (both > 3) to be prime-residue admissible; r(h)=5 if h equiv 2 (mod 6) and r(h)=1 if h equiv 4 (mod 6)
objects.S_h(x): pi(x-h; 6, r(h)) = #{p <= x-h : p prime and p equiv r(h) (mod 6)}
objects.H_2: finite subset of {h >= 2 : h equiv 2 (mod 6), 3 not | h}
objects.H_4: finite subset of {h >= 2 : h equiv 4 (mod 6), 3 not | h}
objects.weights_(w_h): nonneg weights with sum_{h in H_2} w_h = sum_{h in H_4} w_h = 1

definitions.description: Let q in N with 6|q. For even h >= 2:

objects.pi_h(x): #{p <= x-h : p and p+h are prime}
objects.pi(x;q,a): #{p <= x : p prime, p equiv a (mod q)}
objects.pi_h(x;q,a): #{p <= x-h : p equiv a (mod q), p and p+h prime}

definitions.description: Let q be a positive integer with 6|q. For a finite set of even gaps Delta subset 2N with 0 in Delta, write M(Delta) := max Delta.

objects.pi(x;q,a): #{p <= x : p prime, p equiv a (mod q)}
objects.pi_Delta(x;q,a): #{p <= x-M(Delta) : p equiv a (mod q) and p+d is prime for all d in Delta}
objects.F_r(Delta;q): {a in F_r(q) : gcd(a+d,q)=1 for all d in Delta}, fiber-admissible residue sets for r in {1,5}
objects.notation: A(x) <<_{q,Delta} B(x) means |A(x)| <= C(q,Delta) B(x) for some constant C(q,Delta)

conjectures.statement:
For all x >= 7:
|pi_2(x) - pi_4(x)| <= |pi(x;6,5) - pi(x;6,1)|.
In particular, the conjecture predicts that the excess of twin over cousin pairs up to x can never exceed the Chebyshev bias between primes equiv 5 and equiv 1 (mod 6).
Quantitative Refinement (expected under GRH for Dirichlet L-functions mod 6 and Hardy-Littlewood prime-pair asymptotics): There exists absolute K > 0 such that for all x >= 7:
|pi_2(x) - pi_4(x)| <= K * x^{1/2} / log x.

conjectures.name: Start-Normalized Mod-6 Parity Inequality
conjectures.statement:
There exist absolute constants x_0 >= 7 and C > 0 such that for all x >= x_0:
|sum_{h in H_2} w_h * pi_h(x)/S_h(x) - sum_{h in H_4} w_h * pi_h(x)/S_h(x)| <= C/log x (1).
Moreover, for each fixed finite choice of H_2, H_4 and weights, the left-hand side is O((log x)^{-1-delta}) for some delta = delta(H_2,H_4,w) > 0 under GRH for Dirichlet L-functions modulo 6 together with the Hardy-Littlewood prime-pair conjecture for all h in H_2 cup H_4.

conjectures.name: Special Cases and Corollaries
conjectures.statement:
Twin vs cousin (unweighted): take H_2={2}, H_4={4}, w_2=w_4=1, yielding:
|pi_2(x)/pi(x-2;6,5) - pi_4(x)/pi(x-4;6,1)| <= C/log x.
Symmetric 2-gap vs 4-gap families: for any m >= 0, with H_2={6j+2 : 0<=j<=m}, H_4={6j+4 : 0<=j<=m} and uniform weights w_h=1/(m+1):
|(1/(m+1)) sum_{j=0}^m pi_{6j+2}(x)/pi(x-(6j+2);6,5) - (1/(m+1)) sum_{j=0}^m pi_{6j+4}(x)/pi(x-(6j+4);6,1)| <= C/log x.
A simpler, coarser version is |pi_2(x) - pi_4(x)| <= |pi(x-2;6,5) - pi(x-4;6,1)| + O(1).

conjectures.name: Fibered, Start-Normalized Twin-Cousin Near-Equality
conjectures.statement:
There exist constants x_0(q) and C(q) > 0 such that for all x >= x_0(q):
|Pi_2(x;q)/S_5(x;q) - Pi_4(x;q)/S_1(x;q)| <= C(q)/log x (A).
Heuristically, the left-hand side is expected to be <<_q 1/(log x)^2 after cancellation of the common leading term 4C_2/log x.

conjectures.name: Coarse, Unnormalized Envelope by Fibered Chebyshev Bias
conjectures.statement:
For all x >= 7:
|Pi_2(x;q) - Pi_4(x;q)| <= |S_5(x;q) - S_1(x;q)| + 1 (B).
The +1 accounts for the exceptional pair starting at p=3.

conjectures.name: Intra-family Fiber Balance for Sexy Pairs and Chains
conjectures.statement:
For any finite sexy-chain tuple Delta subset 6N with 0 in Delta (e.g., Delta={0,6}), for all sufficiently large x:
|Pi_Delta(x;q,1)/S_1^Delta(x;q) - Pi_Delta(x;q,5)/S_5^Delta(x;q)| <<_{q,Delta} 1/log x.

conjectures.name: Cross-family Coupling Between Complementary Fiber-orientations
conjectures.statement:
Let Delta_2, Delta_4 be finite even-gap tuples with 3 not | d for all d in Delta_2 cup Delta_4 forcing starts in fibers 5 and 1 respectively. For any nonneg weights:
|sum_{d in Delta_2} w_d * Pi_{{0,d}}(x;q,5)/S_5^{{0,d}}(x;q) - sum_{e in Delta_4} v_e * Pi_{{0,e}}(x;q,1)/S_1^{{0,e}}(x;q)| <<_{q,Delta_2,Delta_4} 1/log x.

conjectures.name: Coarse Bias Envelopes (Unnormalized)
conjectures.statement:
For any tuple Delta with both fibers admissible:
|Pi_Delta(x;q,1) - Pi_Delta(x;q,5)| <<_{q,Delta} |S_1^Delta(x;q) - S_5^Delta(x;q)| + 1.

objects.Pi_2(x): #{p <= x : p and p+2 are prime}, twin-prime counting function
objects.Pi_4(x): #{p <= x : p and p+4 are prime}, cousin-prime counting function
objects.pi(x;3,a): #{p <= x : p is prime and p equiv a (mod 3)} for a in {1,2}

open_directions: Prove Conjecture 4.1 under GEH-type hypotheses by adapting dispersion estimates.
Optimize the exponent theta and constant K.
Develop a semiprime proxy inequality by relating Delta_3(x,y) to a smoothed residue-class imbalance for Q in (x,x+y].

conjectures.name: Twin-Cousin Linear Response in Short Intervals
conjectures.statement:
There exist absolute constants x_0 >= 10, theta in (0,1), and K > 0 such that for all x >= x_0 and x^theta <= y <= x:
|Delta_pairs(x,y) - lambda(x) * Delta_3(x,y)| <= K * sqrt(y) / (log x)^2.
Equivalently, a one-sided form holds:
|Delta_pairs(x,y)| <= (2C_2/log x) * |Delta_3(x,y)| + K * sqrt(y) / (log x)^2.

conjectures.name: Cumulative one-variable form
conjectures.statement:
For t >= t_0 (sufficiently large):
|Pi_2(t) - Pi_4(t) - (2C_2/log t) * (pi(t;3,2) - pi(t;3,1))| <= K * sqrt(t) / (log t)^2.

objects.pi_2(x): number of twin-prime pairs (p,p+2) with p+2 <= x
objects.pi_4(x): number of cousin-prime pairs (p,p+4) with p+4 <= x
objects.Q(x): number of semiprimes n <= x (Omega(n)=2, counted with multiplicity)
objects.Delta_h(x): (pi_2(x+h)-pi_2(x)) - (pi_4(x+h)-pi_4(x)), short-interval discrepancy for x >= x_0 and h >= 1

definitions.description: For x >= 2 and h >= 1, let I = (x,x+h]:

objects.pi_2(I): #{p in I : p, p+2 are prime}
objects.pi_4(I): #{p in I : p, p+4 are prime}
objects.P_r(I): #{p in I : p equiv r (mod 12)} for r in {1,5,7,11}

definitions.description: Fix modulus 30. Let R={1,7,11,13,17,19,23,29} be the admissible odd prime residues mod 30. For an interval I=(x,x+h]:

objects.P_r(I): #{p in I prime : p equiv r (mod 30)} for r in R
objects.pi_h(I): #{p in I prime : p+h in I and p+h is prime} for even gaps h in {2,4,6}
objects.A_h(I): sum_{r in S_h} P_r(I) for each h

definitions.description: For k >= 2, let Omega(n) be the total number of prime factors of n with multiplicity. For interval I=(x,x+h]:

objects.Pi_{k,g}(I): #{n in Z cap I : Omega(n)=k and Omega(n+g)=k} for even gap g
objects.A_r^{(k,m)}(I): #{n in Z cap I : Omega(n)=k and n equiv r (mod m)} for residue r in Z/mZ
objects.S_g(I): {r in Z/mZ : A_r^{(k,m)}(I)>0 and A_{r+g}^{(k,m)}(I)>0}, active starts for even gap g
objects.T_g^{(k,m)}(I): sum_{r in S_g(I)} A_r^{(k,m)}(I) * A_{r+g}^{(k,m)}(I), residue-bilinear proxy

conjectures.name: self-normalized square-root law
conjectures.statement:
There exist absolute constants A, B > 0 and theta in (0,1) such that for all x >= x_0 and x^theta <= h <= x:
|Delta_h(x)| <= A sqrt((pi_2(x+h)-pi_2(x)) + (pi_4(x+h)-pi_4(x))) + B.
Equivalently, for the normalized counts:
|(pi_2(x+h)-pi_2(x))/(h/log^2 x) - (pi_4(x+h)-pi_4(x))/(h/log^2 x)| <= A'/sqrt(h/log^2 x) + B'/(h/log^2 x)
with absolute A',B'>0, whenever x^theta <= h <= x.

conjectures.name: semiprime control
conjectures.statement:
There exists an absolute constant C > 0 and theta' in (0,1) such that for all x >= x_0 and x^{theta'} <= h <= x:
|Delta_h(x)| <= C sqrt(Q(x+h) - Q(x)).

conjectures.name: Residue-ratio sandwich for twin vs. cousin pairs
conjectures.statement:
There exists theta in (0,1) and x_0 >= 2 such that for all x >= x_0 and x^theta <= h <= x with pi_4(I) > 0:
min{P_1(I)/P_7(I), P_7(I)/P_1(I)} <= R(I) <= max{P_1(I)/P_7(I), P_7(I)/P_1(I)}.

conjectures.name: Robust form with a universal distortion
conjectures.statement:
There exists an absolute kappa >= 1 such that for all such I:
kappa^{-1} * min{P_1(I)/P_7(I), P_7(I)/P_1(I)} <= R(I) <= kappa * max{P_1(I)/P_7(I), P_7(I)/P_1(I)}.

conjectures.name: Semiprime proxy variant
conjectures.statement:
Let Q_r(I) := #{n in I : n is semiprime and n equiv r (mod 12)}. Then for some theta' in (0,1), x >= x_0', x^{theta'} <= h <= x with pi_4(I) > 0:
min{(Q_1(I)/Q_7(I))^{1/2}, (Q_7(I)/Q_1(I))^{1/2}} <= R(I) <= max{(Q_1(I)/Q_7(I))^{1/2}, (Q_7(I)/Q_1(I))^{1/2}}.

conjectures.name: mod-30 ratio sandwiches for twin, cousin, sexy
conjectures.statement:
There exists a universal distortion kappa >= 1 and theta in (0,1) such that for all x >= x_0 and x^theta <= h <= x, the following hold whenever denominators are nonzero. Let R_{h,k}(I) := pi_h(I)/pi_k(I) and define computable bounds:
L_{h,k}(I) := (mu_h^-(I)/mu_k^+(I)) * (A_h(I)/A_k(I)),
U_{h,k}(I) := (mu_h^+(I)/mu_k^-(I)) * (A_h(I)/A_k(I)).
Then for (h,k) in {(2,4),(2,6),(4,6)} one has:
kappa^{-1} L_{h,k}(I) <= R_{h,k}(I) <= kappa U_{h,k}(I).

conjectures.name: Semiprime proxy (prime-free)
conjectures.statement:
For Q_r(I) := #{n in I : n is semiprime and n equiv r (mod 30)}, set tilde_P_r(I) := sqrt(Q_r(I)), and define tilde_A_h, tilde_mu_h^+/- analogously. There exists kappa_Q >= 1 such that:
kappa_Q^{-1} tilde_L_{h,k}(I) <= R_{h,k}(I) <= kappa_Q tilde_U_{h,k}(I),
where tilde_L_{h,k}, tilde_U_{h,k} are defined from tilde_P_r exactly as L_{h,k}, U_{h,k} are from P_r.

conjectures.name: Residue-shift ratio sandwich for k-almost primes
conjectures.statement:
There exists, for each k >= 2 and modulus m, a universal distortion kappa_k(m) >= 1 such that for any interval I=(x,x+h] with Pi_{k,g'}(I) > 0, and any even gaps g,g' (e.g., 2,4,6), the two-variable ratio:
R_{k;g,g'}(I) := Pi_{k,g}(I) / Pi_{k,g'}(I)
satisfies:
kappa_k(m)^{-1} * L_{k;g,g'}^{(m)}(I) <= R_{k;g,g'}(I) <= kappa_k(m) * U_{k;g,g'}^{(m)}(I),
where the computable envelopes are:
L_{k;g,g'}^{(m)}(I) := (mu_g^-(I)/mu_{g'}^+(I)) * (A_g^{(k,m)}(I)/A_{g'}^{(k,m)}(I)),
U_{k;g,g'}^{(m)}(I) := (mu_g^+(I)/mu_{g'}^-(I)) * (A_g^{(k,m)}(I)/A_{g'}^{(k,m)}(I)).
Heuristic: Pi_{k,g}(I) is comparable to T_g^{(k,m)}(I) (up to kappa_k(m)) because the event {Omega(n)=k, Omega(n+g)=k} factorizes weakly across residue fibers modulo m. The same leading singular weights appear inside each g when restricted to a fixed residue, while their cross-g variation is absorbed into the universal kappa_k(m).
